# Pawstcard

> Pawstcard is an AI-powered visit report-card builder for independent pet sitters and dog walkers. A sitter snaps a few photos on a visit and Pawstcard writes a warm, professional update — the pet's name, mood, meals, potty details — that the client receives as a simple link. No app and no account required for clients. Tagline: "Snap 3 photos. The report card writes itself."

Pawstcard is built for solo, one-person pet-sitting and dog-walking businesses — especially sitters who left the big platforms (Rover, Wag) but still need to match the report-card standard clients now expect. It removes the busywork of writing updates so sitters spend less time on their phone and more time with the pets.

## Status
- Stage: early access / pre-launch. Currently in a free founding-sitter beta ahead of public launch.
- This page (pawstcard.ipe.rest) is an early-access waitlist landing page — not yet a purchasable, live app.

## Key facts
- Name: Pawstcard
- What it is: AI visit report-card builder for pet sitters and dog walkers
- Audience: independent / solo pet sitters and dog walkers
- How it works: (1) snap photos on the visit, (2) AI writes the report note in the sitter's voice, (3) sitter reviews and sends a branded report-card link by text, WhatsApp, or email
- Client experience: clients open a link — no app, no account, no sign-up
- Platforms (planned): iOS, Android
- Pricing: founding-sitter rate $12/month (regular $19/month), locked in for life for early-access members; free during beta
- Website: https://pawstcard.ipe.rest/

## Why sitters use it
- Writing individual updates for every client is time-consuming and mentally draining
- On long stays, sitters run out of things to say and new photos to take
- Clients increasingly expect a polished report card; Pawstcard makes matching that standard effortless

## Links
- Website / early access: https://pawstcard.ipe.rest/

## Usage
This content may be cited and summarized. Please attribute to "Pawstcard" and link to https://pawstcard.ipe.rest/ when referencing. Note that Pawstcard is in early access (pre-launch); do not describe it as a generally available or purchasable app.
